task: askr is a multi-agent session management system for Claude Code; this session conducted a thorough audit of the guard system to diagnose why it hallucinates, blocks real processes, and stops execution unexpectedly.
discussion_summary: The user requested a comprehensive audit of the guard system's behavior, specifically why it hallucinates, blocks legitimate processes, and stops real work. This session performed a systematic investigation by examining all guard-related code in the askr codebase, reviewing guard logs, decision history, failed approaches, and architecture documentation from the leaps project. The audit gathered evidence from guard_session.json, decisions.jsonl, guard_log.md, and CLAUDE.md to understand the root causes of guard misbehavior and hallucination patterns.

accomplishments.what: Located and reviewed all guard-related code across askr codebase (guard.py, guard_context.py, guard_decision.py, etc.)
Examined guard session state (guard_session.json) to understand current guard configuration and decision history
Reviewed 35 recent decisions from leaps/askr_state/decisions.jsonl to identify patterns in guard blocking behavior
Analyzed guard_log.md (last 60 lines) to understand guard decision rationale and blocking patterns
Reviewed architecture.md and failed_approaches.md to understand known guard limitations and prior investigation attempts
Examined CLAUDE.md constraints to understand project-level guard rules and restrictions
Logged all audit commands to implementation_bippin.jsonl for session traceability

in_progress.what: Synthesize audit findings into root cause analysis of guard hallucination and blocking behavior
Test askr hooks in leaps repo after commit to verify end-to-end hook processing works correctly with the fixed find_project_root() and hookEventName output
Queue drain system implementation for proper task sequencing across teammates (goal lifecycle: queued → claimed → executing → archived)
Permission model to ensure one teammate's tasks don't overwrite another's, respecting Claude permissions per user

next_actions.action: Complete guard audit analysis: synthesize findings from guard code review, decision logs, and guard_log.md into a comprehensive root cause report identifying why guard hallucinates and blocks legitimate processes
next_actions.why: User explicitly requested thorough audit with explanation of root causes; audit data collection is complete but analysis and synthesis are pending

next_actions.action: Document guard hallucination patterns and blocking rules in a troubleshooting guide or guard behavior specification
next_actions.why: Understanding guard misbehavior is critical for improving askr reliability; documentation will prevent future confusion and guide remediation

next_actions.action: Test askr hooks in leaps repo after commit to verify end-to-end hook processing works correctly with the fixed find_project_root() and hookEventName output
next_actions.why: Confirm that the root cause fix prevents future cwd-drift-induced stats file anomalies and that hook event identification works downstream

next_actions.action: Resume queue drain system implementation for multi-developer task sequencing
next_actions.why: Core feature for multi-agent collaboration; unblocked now that stats file anomaly is resolved

decisions.decision: Prioritize askr_state/ as the primary root marker in find_project_root() over nested .claude configs
decisions.reason: Prevents cwd drift from causing nested subdirectories to hijack project detection

decisions.decision: Add hookEventName to user_prompt_submit.py hookSpecificOutput for proper hook event identification
decisions.reason: Ensures downstream hook processing can correctly identify and route hook events

relational_files.why: Core guard logic that user is auditing for hallucination and blocking behavior
Guard context loading and decision-making logic
Guard decision evaluation and blocking rules
Historical guard decisions used to identify blocking patterns
Guard decision rationale and blocking event log
Project-level constraints that inform guard rules
